steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to connect Square Tower House to modern-day Pueblo buildings. The bridge in the notes: Square Tower House was built with adobe and stone (1200–1300 CE), and modern Pueblo buildings like those in Zuni Pueblo and Isleta Pueblo still use adobe and stone alongside contemporary elements.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Modern-day Pueblo buildings, such as those in Zuni Pueblo and Isleta Pueblo, incorporate elements that wouldn't have been found in Square Tower House.’: This highlights differences, not a connection. It works against the stated goal. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The Ancestral Puebloan settlement of Square Tower House was built with adobe and stone, materials still used in the buildings of Zuni Pueblo and Isleta Pueblo.’: Directly links Square Tower House to modern Pueblo buildings through the shared use of adobe and stone. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘The elaborate buildings of Square Tower House in southwestern Colorado incorporate both traditional and modern-day elements in their design.’: Misattributes modern elements to Square Tower House (a 13th-century site); it also fails to mention any modern Pueblo buildings. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Square Tower House, located in southwestern Colorado, was inhabited from approximately 1200–1300 CE, but its buildings aren't actively occupied today.’: States historical facts but makes no connection to modern Pueblo buildings. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
